lines.x: Fifteen knights, fifteen chances to take first prize!
Who's the Knight tonight?! Who are you betting on?! And who's going to walk out of the bloodbath with a handful of gold? Who'll stride through the storm to win our admiration?!
We're ten minutes away from the official start of the match! Don't miss this chance to get filthy rich overnight! You'd only end up kicking yourself! Now let's witness the birth of a rising star!
What's your name?
Do you need something?
C'mon, drop the act for a minute. It's not every day you see a Zalak knight around here, so I'm just popping over to say hi, that's all.
Nervous?
Are you... Infected too?
I mean, who wouldn't be? Only Infected would fight a match like this... our lives are a goddamned game here.
What'cha staring at me for? Spooky.
It's just... you seem a little... familiar.
Mmm? Trying to cozy up to me?
No. Maybe you're right about Zalak knights... being pretty uncommon.
Well, since life's bundled us together, how about we team up?
Huh?
Quit playing around. I'm in this to—
To give the knight nobility a little hands-on knock-knock?
...How do you...
Got it in your eyes.
It's too obvious. There's disdain, there's anger, and they've been your bywords for a little too long—those couple of bigshot knights up in the stands, they're beginning to notice you, y'know?
This place isn't worth that.
Don't jump to conclusions, okay... take a look around. What are the different kinds of Infected knights around here?
Some bought up by the big shots as a little gambling pastime. There's a share in it for them if they make it, and if they lose, hoo boy, let's not get into that.
Of course, you've got some anonymous nobodies, thirsty for a real-ass high-riding knight or some merchant to pick them out. Thirsty to become... a tool.
They've got no other way. Infected never get any other way.
What are you getting at?
C'mon. Let's team up, okay?
Your chances in a melee, see, they're always better with a little personal alliancing, y'know?
So why do you want to win, then? For the prize money?
No. Because I can't let anyone else win.
—The hell.
There's something going on behind this match. A couple Infected... I'm not too sure yet, but my worry's they've 'settled on it,' so to speak.
They've decided to trample the others, haul themselves on up, for better odds of survival.
The only real choice, right? Too real for plenty here to not be thinking it.
—That's why I can't let them win. If someone wins, someone loses. Big whoop. But if someone's living, then... someone's dying.
A vague sense of justice isn't gonna win you much trust.
But—sure. Right now is make-or-break for Knights Association approval...
I'm in. Zalak and Zalak. Beats teaming with some rando any day.
Eh? For real?
If you just came over to screw with me, then I'm gonna start with you.
Whoa, whoa, whoa, no, I'm real. Let's take this melee together, Ashley.
Greynuty Kaliska.
...Kaliska...?
Joke of a surname. Just pretend you don't know, even if you've heard it before.
Now! Let's welcome—
You can call me Sona, Ashley.
...Sona.
Mmm? What's up?
Back then, at the end, you took my points out of nowhere, didn't you?
...Ahaha... Ashley, c'mon... that was, uh...
C'mon, are you still not over that?
Don't you think I might be a little stuck on it?
Well...
But not because you stole the crown from me.
Uh...
You said you couldn't let anyone else win... and I was just the same.
Urgh!
What are you overreacting for? We'd only just met then. It's normal not to trust.
No, well... uh... actually, yeah...
Look, weren't we going to see how the Infected ghetto was doing? Let's move.
Allow me! To come up with an excuse! To restore our bonds of Knightclub trust—!
Since when were we in a Knightclub?!
...Heh.
Since sooner or later, Ashley.
You mean... that Knightclub for Infected?
Pinus Sylvestris.
I came from around Victoria, if you're curious. Plenty of that sort of pine tree around, back when I was young. These days they're long gone, though.
...Pinus sylvestris? Pinus... sylvestris...
You were one of those Zalaks who got hit by that Catastrophe?! You got left for dead by the knights too—the Kaliskas abandoned you...
Let it go, Ashley. It's bygone stuff.
But... then your infection was... my family's fault?
Didn'tcha already break from your family? They don't count as family anymore, then.
You're not feeling any guilt for me, not on my watch. Oh, right, weren't you just talking about how I stole your championship?
We're even.
So you're... 'Flametail' Sona?
You know me, then.
Haha, not many Infected around who win knight titles. I know them all.
So Pinus Sylvestris is led by the one and only 'Flametail' Sona... that puts me at ease, then.
You look after those kids?
I do. Er, but I only took them in... and a couple of them aren't Infected, actually. I was gonna send them to an aid station somewhere.
So I can't come along for the ride. Sorry, Flametail.
Can I get your name, at least?
...Jamie. Call me Jamie.
Jamie, then. You said you know a couple Infected knights?
Every Infected keeping up the independent fight is an ally in waiting.
I remember one making a big splash lately... a Liberi, a sniper.
You're right, and there's a Kuranta... she's 'Leader' to a couple Infected knights, and to others, 'Wild Mane.'
...*Spit*.
Yo, Iwona. Tired?
Hey there, Sona.
You, uh... seem pretty smooth rolling, still. Strong stuff.
Not really. Just... always gotta keep up some of the lax act.
You tired too? Didn't know you felt it. Figured you were always like that, on the forward march.
Ahaha...
Hah. That proves you trust me, though. Looking that beat in front of me.
So, this, uh... Armorless Union biz. How do we deal with 'em?
Do everything we can to keep them from knowing where exactly the Infected are settled.
So long as they hide in the city, an Infected's got nowhere to go.
We'll get found sooner or later, Sona. This isn't gonna solve anything.
You need to think of some way—
Mmm.
—Nah, maybe we oughtn'ta dump everything on your back. I'm sorry.
Yeah, chewing over stuff like this isn't my style, but you oughtn'ta run your own load too heavy, Sona. We're here for you.
Mmm!
She's an Infected, isn't she? How'd she get so many points...?
Not just that, Night of Knights had a whole spread for her interview a few days ago!
They either take underground fights for money, or uncompenasted battles just to get a title from the Association. How do Infected like her even survive?
So that's Justyna?
She looks frail as a twig. Where's the skill?
You ever hear the one about books and covers?
But the papers haven't, uh... been duly proper to her lately.
How many times have they ever been proper to Infected?
Actually, forget Infected. When have the media ever cared about being fair to anyone?
Can we believe anything that Armorless goon said?
It's something to go off. Beats having no ideas at all.
What's our plan?
That's what I'm talking about—Justyna! Get that Feather Knight!
To the left! Behind the stone bunker! Yeah! That's it—no, no! Don't peek out, you'll be clocked—nooo!
She's pretty popular, huh.
Might not wanna join us?
If she keeps doing what she's doing, she'll be titled sooner or later. Make a killing riding the wave of popularity, and maybe she can go find somewhere quiet to live out her days.
If she's that kind of Infected, can we really talk them into helping us?
We can't talk anyone over.
But this city forces Infected to make a choice in the end.
Honestly, it might just force everyone.
I hardly ever see you this pessimistic.
You're the one who asked!
I'm here hoping the Flametail Knight'll declare to me 'no problem, it's all under control.'
...haha.
No problem... it's all under control.
The Adeptus Sprawiedliwi?!
Shh—
...Still not certain.
Besides, it's still ambiguous how the Adeptus treat Infected.
How would any of those knight families give a thought for the Infected?
We're just outsiders to them. They'd never let us get away.
No matter how arrogant they are, I'd still take five hundred of them over the K.G.C.C. Dumb shameless assholes.
You've got a soft spot for campaign knights.
Ha, they're the knights who fought Ursus off the grasslands! Tops clowns on stage winning points with the audience any day!
...Ursus. All I know of them is the news I saw when I was young...
Ursus lost their war with Higashi. Of course the knights cranked up the publicity machine.
You... know about the war between Ursus and Higashi too?
Hey, they forced me to read a dead tree or two when I was young. It was all stuff on the modern history of the campaign knights.
I can't imagine it.
Iwona sitting at a table, holding a book. It's too farfetched. Inconceivable.
What?
You're from a campaign knight family, I think?
Studying's not... outta the question... for you, I guess, haha...
Keh! Is this how you all see me?!
Ten years after the Tenth Kazimierz-Ursus War, the Emperor of Ursus got seriously sick, and everything annexed from Kazimierz fell into the nobles' hands.
The crown prince picked up on the corruption inside the military even before succession, goading two Armies to rise up, as a distraction.

No way the Ursus nobles could ever agree on one plan, but they were all born and raised in this nation of chaos. They sent their own forces out against Higashi without any argument, so they could cover up their own crimes...
Which led to Higashi launching an attack of their own on Ursus far beyond what anyone expected, and in the end, the decisive battle took place inside Ursus territory.
Jeez. Didn't know you had straight history lectures in you, Iwona.
No clarification on those bits that obviously got mixed in.
At that point in Kazimierz, a good deal of the knights thought they should strike while Ursus was up to their ears in it, and in all the unrest, they'd take back the cities they lost ten years ago...
Iwona?
...and that was 1072. 25 years ago.
My grandparents were there with the scouts who resisted Ursus, only to get strangled by the Army before they could really rise up. I wish I got to know them.
Hey, don't take it so hard. That's the past. I'm not a campaign knight anymore, so I jump when you say jump, Sona.
Enough about me. Let's talk your plans now.
This morning, Jamie got three, maybe four new Infected in on his end.
Miners, infected in a small industrial accident, but the contractor declined to reimburse their medical fees, so with nowhere to go, they ended up with the police out for them.
And that's not the end... we've had over ten normal Infected join us this month already, right?
They live in the darkest, dankest corners of the Grand Knight Territory... we've got no way to settle them.
...Sona.
We've still got time. We've got power. We'll find a way.
But... I won't... let any innocent Infected end up risking themselves with us.
If the Adeptus really will let us all have legal identities again, live on in Kazimierz, then—

lines.st: We have no other choice.

lines.x: —Man, this is some party.
Flametail Knight, Sona.
You are suspected of illegally harboring Infected, publicly violating the Knights Association's code of conduct. Put an end to your resistance.
Hand over the illegal Infected, and we will report your case to the Knights Association.
Musta missed this. Armorless have authority to help settle biz for the police and knights now?
Let's all be honest. Don't you guys feel ashamed when you use the law as this big pompous excuse?
Your provocations will get you nowhere, Flametail Knight.
You have already overstepped the mark. Here in the Grand Knight Territory, drawing the Chamber's gaze is tantamount to death.
Death? That's some scary stuff there.
A biological death is not death's only sense, Flametail Knight.
The more chances for survival Kazimierz accords us, the more deaths it creates for us down the line.
All hands, take aim!
—Shelling from the rear?!
Sona! I'm here!
It's backup from Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub! Surround them!
Justyna!
Roger. Laying cover fire.
Sorry, Sona, I'm going in!
Do Infected cry?
What?
Infected. I don't mean the knights who had the guts to resist the Chamber. I mean all the normal Infected.
They just got Oripathy, right?
Er... yes?
They're not monsters. And they're not scum of the earth either.
I'm not all that into this assignment.
Don't ever let the Lazurites hear you say that!
But still, have you found some sort of compassion for them, Platinum?
Huh. Compassion... not really, I guess. I don't even get the time for myself, after all.
Compassion's a luxury kinda deal these days.
I've got other assignments. Dealing with the Infected is pretty small-time. You guys have it.
It doesn't matter what tantrums Adeptus throws at the K.G.C.C., neither's gonna wanna keep Infected in the Grand Knight Territory.
The nobles aren't gonna care whether Infected live or die, are they?
...How the...
Flametail!
It's the Armorless... the Armorless suddenly attacked us!
Stay calm. What went down?
They sealed off the street nearby two hours ago. We were busted long ago. And then they... used gas to force us out of the houses...!
A few knights picked up on it. They used their own bodies to block the Armorless's shots, but some still...
Where...?
Where do we go next? If the Armorless Union got us the first time, they'll get us the second, the third... we...
Save the eulogies. First, we need to... move the rest of the Infected.
Should we get out of the Grand Knight Territory? Go somewhere in the countryside...
At least! At least get the Infected civilians out of here—
We've been leaning on our knighthood so hard to give them some kind of cover.
When there's no knighthood involved, when they're out of the Grand Knight Territory, it's only gonna get worse for them...
So we just... take it?! We give up and accept this?!
...The Adeptus.
We still have... that on the table, don't we.
Sona!
I'm back.
How was it?
I was dealing with an old knight... Definitely one of the Adeptus higher-ups, no doubt about it.
Best we don't trust the Grand Knight Territory nobles too hard, Sona.
It was Demian... vice-president of the Knights Association.
Ha, there's a big guy for you. VP Demian, the renowned campaign knight.
I remember him. Huge ruckus for the news when he was discharged and came back to the Territory to do boring old civil service.
A descendant of the heroes, like that one Maria girl.
And the conditions...?
If you're back here, that means there's been actual headway, correct? What conditions did the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi set?
A separation. A Great Separation.
He wants to borrow our efforts... to put this city... to sleep.
...Uh. What's that mean?
That's how the plan goes, roughly.
We can do this... right?
Are you asking me?
Maybe. I'm not giving up until I see those dumb knight nobles bowing to me.
You guys?
Real simple plan. We fail if we're not strong enough. We get even stronger next time, we're good.
Long as we don't die, yeah?
...Relax. Uncoil, don't get nervous.
Pinus Sylvestris just needs to stay Pinus Sylvestris... and we'll be fine.
Whether or not we succeed, we only have one way open to us. There's no difference, really.
...Difference, huh.
Sona!
Hm?
You've been zoning out a lot lately. What's wrong?
Nah... nothing, really.
Let's go. We've gotta find a place for the Infected, still.
Run away... hurry!
Away from the sawdust... fire everywhere... chokes your sinuses... the soil's too hot to touch...
Ore... Originium... gashing...
The Kaliskas abandoned their subjects—
(Did I... fall asleep? On a bench?)
Ashley'd be on my case again if she was sitting here.
Phew...
Lot of people... out on the street.
(Infected under the Knights Association's supervision aren't allowed to walk around freely.)
(If all these pedestrians... knew there was an Infected out in broad moonlight here, I wonder what they'd think?)
Without knowing why, a thought flits through Sona's mind.
'They wouldn't think much.'
Pedestrians bustle, coming and going as life demands, melting together under the rainbow lights.
The Zalak called 'Flametail' silently watches the city before her.
Hm? Oh, hey, it's Ashley. What's up—
Trouble! Sona, they found the underground shelter!
We're not gonna make it in time to move everyone! Whoever we save, we save! Hurry, get back—
This is an Infected...
Right here!
Where are we going, Granny?
It's not the right time for you to ask.
Hold tight to your pouch, and stay close to me.
Yes, Granny.
We're almost there.
What's that there?
An arena. Built by bastards from the Grand Knight Territory.
Humph.
Are we going to see a match?
A match? No.
I'm taking you for training.
Do you still remember the things I taught you?
Yes, Granny, I haven't forgotten any!
It's shield up, cannon up, and bombard!
Eyes on the enemy, watch your ammunition, don't let your cannon go!
And, um...
When the cannon fire rings through the air, victory is already within sight!
Very good.
You're a lot more dependable than your father.
At least someone else will remember these before I close my eyes for good.
Please don't be so gloomy, Granny...
Heh. A tree may be a century old, but its roots will all be rotten.
Better to be fertilizer for the new buds than to wither away like this.
*cough* *cough*...
Granny!
It's all right, it was just a little cough. Nothing to worry about.
Your father's generation. Even my few brothers. They've all long forgotten how the Kaliskas came to prosper.
Forestry? That forest was given back to us upon our high deeds, upon our cannon fire!
'When the cannon fire rings through the air, victory is already within sight'... All those bastards, with all their money, can't even remember the saying!
Granny, please don't get angry...
Hmph...
Greynuty, you're a good sprout.
Don't grow twisted.
Yes, Granny, I won't disappoint you!
The Bloodboil Knightclub's—Butcher of the Field—back after nursing those injuries—as ruthless as ever!!
And on—the other—side—
*cough*, *cough* *cough*...
Once the foresting magnate of the south—a name out of the Kaliska Group—
But now an Infected—part of the Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub—
Let's give a big warm welcome to—
Granny?
(Huff... relax. Granny already taught me. I can do this.)
(I'm ready!)
The Ashlock Knight, Greynuty Kaliska!
Man, oh, man, Ashley finally takes the field.
She's never in a hurry. Never gives the audience the time of day, either. Reveling just isn't Greynuty.
That's her selling point. Some people are suckers for that. Look over there.
Greynuty! Greynuty, look at me!!!
Eh... yeah, it works.
There's no faking cheers.
And that posture, the hunker with the cannon, it's got noble written between the lines.
Course I'm still most excited for her to fire off, though.
Well, you're gonna get that. The crowd's gone wild hearing the rumbles.
Just wish there was more to get a kick out of.
What, you want her bashing with the barrel?
Haha. Man, I hope I see it, if it ever happens.
Humongous hatchet meets sturdy shield! Who'll come out on top? We'll just have to wait and see!
Switch your terminals on, make your choice, and give your competitor your support!
And before we forget, the most important part—grab that chance to stuff your wallet full!
Let the match—begiiiiiinnnnnnnn—!
You'd best hope that hunk of plastic holds out.
Oripathic filth.
Baah, that's dull. She's not even saying anything.
This isn't your first time watching Ashley compete. It's called being serious. D'ja know what being serious is?
But this is mad boring... if you don't vent your feelings, how're you gonna keep momentum against your opponent?
Knight competitions aren't yelling contests, jeez. The important thing is to look good standing off.
See, just like that. Brassrust's getting forced back, and everyone's on cloud nine, right?
You don't think this match is too calm, though? Barely any active attacks.
You've gotta pick your opponents. With knights like Brassrust who go all in on offense, you wanna put together a good defense to be optimal.
And Ashley can use her favorite move now.
Block with a shield bash, get a shot in. Tried and tested.
(Ashley doesn't seem that hungry to attack this match. What's on her mind, huh?)
It's a shocker! The Butcher's cleaver has nowhere to go before the Ashlock Knight!
Each time he attacks! Each blast he takes! Ingra gives, but he gets nothing!
The Ashlock Knight's defense is perfect, and she's making every step forward hers, squeezing down the space the Butcher has to move!
Without a doubt, this is a battle of back and forth! We won't know who'll wear the victory laurel until the final moment!
Get your terminals out and lay your bets on your knight of choice!
Or buy a water bottle with your contestant's logo straight away, specially provided by the Major, and let the crowd around you know just who you support!
Order now! We're ready to hook you up, right there in your seats!
That's something they do now?
They do, but, uh...
These prices... why don't I just step out of the arena and come back with ten bottles?
Haha...
You wouldn't order one...
Just one. Just to try, heheh.
Can you do anything besides shrink behind that plastic board, hmm?
Hah. You'll have time to scream once I shatter it, rock-festered mouse.
A petty firm like the Kaliskas would never collect your corpse.
(Pulls trigger)
Whoa, Ashley's pissed now!
Ingra's being too cruel, though. What's he keep yanking her sore spots for?
Kaliska Forestry used to be pretty well-known, but you don't hear much about it in the news lately.
Can't be something to do with Greynuty.
If they had her doing things, they might still be as big as ever.
Doing piss-poor management yourself, taking it out on the others. Not a recipe for company success.
I heard it was at the table she got expelled from the family. Facing her uncles and aunts. Voted out by all.
Unanimous passage...
So her mom and dad...
Yeah, I hear blackmail had them throwing their own daughter out.
Had the funds for Ashley to head Grand Knight Territory-ways later, though.
But'cha probably figure, y'know. There's your parents, and everything sensible says they oughta be with you. And they put their hands up too...
That's just gotta hurt.
Good thing Ashley's strong enough stuff.
Especially strong.
Uh... she can't be pissed for REAL real.
Lemme look—
We're good, we're good.
It's normal enough to be mad at a time like this, right?
For sure, I'd ream a hole straight through Brassrust before thinking about the rest.
Ashley takes fights too cerebral, and ammo runs out. If she fires a round throwing a fit, she might just be boned.
Still oughta be the phase where she makes Brassrust THINK she's angry.
After all, misleading your opponent's important too.
Look.
I'm floored, and I bet you are too! Ingra's shoulder plates have been blasted to smithereens!
The Ashlock Knight is still pressing forward step by step! Could victory be at hand? Is the proclamation coming?!
Another crack of shellfire! It looks like the Ashlock Knight's got her own personal victory salute covered!
What's this?! Did you see that, everyone?!
Brassrust just cleaved a shell apart with his hatchet! We'd be remiss not to thank our two contestants! You only get spectacles like this on the soaps!

That's right, I'm talking about the classic scene from the Słoma-funded suspense-in-the-city production, 24 Nights in the G.K.T.! Anyone in the audience interested, you can put an order in for the discs straight away!
The first thirty to buy'll get a poster signed by the two leads! First come, first served! Don't miss out!
Glug, glug—phew. Man, this is basically the same as convenience store water.
Didn't I warn you already...
Heehee. Whatever, let's just say I bought it for her.
Forget that. Mob never misses a chance to shill, huh. Really gets on your nerves...
That's his job, isn't it? What else would he do?
Who thought Brassrust could cut a shell clean through, though? Really forced an ad spot right into Mob's lap.
If only Ashley didn't go soft for a moment. Wanted to knock Brassrust down, didn't aim for his head...
Match would've been over by now.
Her magazine's gotta be drying up.
Mmm.
Only one shot left, I think.
Time to swap mags.
Here's hoping Ingra doesn't give our Ashley any trouble then.
(One round left.)
(Gotta push him off, hop back on the recoil, and that should give me enough time.)
(With Ingra's competition style, he shouldn't have any out.)
(This is the moment.)
Is that all you can do?
(He sped up?)
(Shit!)
Is that a—thruster on Brassrust's shoes?
There's a wrench in the plan...
You've got this, Ashley!
*cough*...
You're out of shells. What else can you do?
D'ya still have ammo, Ashley?
None. I just used the reserve to block the bolts.
What now, then? Gonna bash with the barrel?
If you need it, I can make a distraction for you.
And then ride the opportunity to take my points like last time?
C'mon, let that go already...
We'd only just met then. Be a problem not having your guard up.
I won't do it again. We agreed on that.
I can trust you not to sacrifice other Infected for your own benefit, Greynuty.
So you can trust Sona here not to stab you in the back.
Right?
Hmph. I suppose.
So how are you gonna face down these knights now?
I can definitely make you a little space. Shout if you need me.
I won't.
I've got my methods.
Fresh from the advantage, the Ashlock Knight has been caught out reloading! Brassrust begins his revenge! Could the Butcher's cleaver taste blood when all's said and done?!
Just how is the Ashlock Knight gonna counter? There she goes—she's thrown her empty magazine at Brassrust, but it's had no—
Oh, wait a moment, sister, she's thrown her cannon down too and is charging Brassrust! What is she up to?!

Ingra's got his weapon up, and the Ashlock Knight only has a shield to her name! Ashlock—Ashlock has blocked the battleaxe, and—ooh!! She's clocked him straight in the head! I heard that all the way from here!
Ooh! Back-to-back and hand-to-hand! It's a barrage! Ingra's on the retreat!
Greynuty's rolled right back to her weapon, swapped and loaded! Not a beat missed! Is Ingra in for another artillery lockdown?!
Ah-ha! We're once again treated to the sound of cannon fire! The Ashlock Knight has the situation back under control!
Might be a little ugly, but... that's a pretty practical way to disengage.
Never dreamt she'd go so fast once she dropped that cannon, though.
Ashley's a Zalak too, y'know. A little agility's par for our course.
So why won't she use a light weapon like yours?
Personal preference, I guess.
I suggested she could switch to a more arena-friendly long-ranger—javelins, crossbows, slings, stuff like that. She's not a fan of any of them. Just likes using that old family cannon.
She can't even put it to full use sometimes, and the maintenance is a pain.
That's stubborn, alright.
You get used to it, y'know.
I think she's down to her last eight rounds.
If she runs clean out, what happens?
I've got no idea. What DOES happen?
I've never seen Ashley use up all her reserve ammo before.
...*pant*, *pant*—Are you toying with me, Ashlock?!
Say something—you damned—rock!
(Pulls trigger)
How long do you plan on playing the mute?!
I've had enough—enough of this!
Hrreaaagh!!
Man, Ashley really is another level of horrible.
There's no way she was planning a tug of war with Ingra from the start. Was she?
She doesn't respond, she doesn't go down. She'd get me mad too.
Why?
Oh, Justyna, you made it.
Why is Ashlock bent on humiliating Brassrust?
Thaaat's a long story.
When I ran into Ashley, she was stoked on anger, wrath for all the old knight lords at the top of the stands.
The other Infected knights thought she just resented the old guard, pure and simple.
But if—
If she wasn't an Infected, she'd be a knight noble too.
I've heard.
Post-infection, no-one her generation or above in her knight family showed her any sympathy.
Everyone put the blame for the family's slow fall on her.
Normal people would know that's nothing to do with getting ill.
But that's what they did. Her family, every Kaliska Zalak agreed to strip Ashley of her position and boot her out of the lineage.
She used to trust all the family, so much. She loved sharing fun stories with them. Comparing techniques with them.
But in the end, no one she thought a friend reached out a hand to help.
I heard some were looking to have her disappeared altogether—
It—can't be that bad.
One day, I was there. Helped keep them off Ashley.
So you can guess why Ashley has such a bitter hatred for the old knight lords.
As for Brassrust Ingra...
Every single thing he does is a deep sting on Ashley's heart.
That's what's going on. I'm guessing it's why she was ready to stand off in a close contest until the end.
To bring a lofty, high-ranking knight straight into the ground.
And to... keep her own anger in check.
Hey, Justyna, can you tell how many shells Ashley has left in her mag for me?
Four.
Ooh.
Nearly closing time.
A fierce offensive has not toppled the Ashlock Knight! And Brassrust is beginning to wear out!
He's readying for his final charge!
This marathon back-and-forth is about to pay off!
Ladies and gentlemen, knights and dames, keep an eye on your betting numbers, please! Who knows if you're suddenly in for a prize when the results come in!

This post-match lottery is sponsored by Roar Guards Co., and the rates will boggle your mind! An astonishing zero point FIVE percent! If you still haven't placed your bets, you have one last chance now!
Ingra has lifted his hatchet, the blade of the abattoir!
Greynuty assumes a defensive position!
We're about to see who wins and who loses!
Jeez, Louise! When'd Ashley make off with my moves?!
Brassrust is charging, he's still charging, Ashlock has her shield up, but she's pointed the barrel backwards!
Could she be taking Brassrust for a head-on impact?!
Both are coming in hot, red-yellow-white-hot!
What?!
Ashlock's accelerated with her shelling and has sailed right by Brassrust's hatchet!
Too fast, way too fast!!
She's ditched her shield, the barrel's against Ingra's back, she's pulled the trigger!!
The Brassrust Knight is down! The Ashlock Knight has taken the win!!!
Hold on, now Ashlock is aiming the barrel at Brassrust again! Could it be? The day the tyrannical Ingra is humiliated himself?!
...Rgh. A Zalak shrunken in a shell.
Ashlock... Greynuty... I'll remember... you.
When I'm healed... you... you Infected—will not live to tell one tale!
—You!
(Pulls the trigger)
Kgh...
(Aims barrel at the sky)
This shell is an offering to the spectators.
You don't deserve it.
You... urgh...
The Ashlock Knight has not dealt a closing blow to Ingra! With a final blast of a shell, she's left alone for the exit!
Greynuty! Greynuty—!
This match's—lottery—prize—
How did you feel on that run?
Not that good, Father. My hand still gets tremors holding the cannonlance for too long.
Do you want to switch to a lighter weapon?
No. The cannonlance suits me. Its might makes up for what I lack in flexibility.
And besides, someday, I might not be fighting in the arena, but somewhere much vaster.
Just like Grandmother.
When that time comes, this weapon will surely be much more practical.
Alright... as you wish.
My little nut.
Father, I'm not a child anymore. You don't need to call me that...
Fair.
Greynuty.
You did well in training.
If Grandma could see the strides you've made, I'm sure she'd be delighted.
Grandmother...
Before Grandma left, she made an agreement with some of the uncles. As long as you're willing to learn, they'll continue to pay your fees.
So you don't need to worry about money.
Whether or not you become a knight in the end, or inherit the family business.
Keep practicing, and there'll be a day you become a famed cannonlancer of Kazimierz.
Ah, how many generations it's been since the Kaliskas last had a true cannonlancer.
All your ancestors would be proud of you.
Don't let them down.
Yes, Father.
I won't.
Hey, Ashley. Ashley?
Um—muh?
What's the matter?
What were you daydreaming about?
Nothing. Just thinking.
Poor-ass Ingra. Just came out of the hospital and you send him right back.
He deserves it.
And you still fired your last round in the air?
The match was already over. Even if I can't stand the sight of Ingra, I won't do anything else to him.
Otherwise, what would separate me and him?
Shame, I was looking forward to a 'Butcher Suffers Slaughter'-type headline in the papers...
Chances are the Ingra family wouldn't let that news onto the papers at all.
Sona, I've actually been meaning to ask you for a little bit now...
Hm? What's up, Ashley?
What are you—holding there?
An Ashlock water bottle.
Why would you buy something so useless?
It worked out. I was thirsty, I couldn't be bothered to go out to buy water.
Yoo. Our knight returns triumphant.
I'll have to learn from you too, Ashlock, and become stronger.
Justyna, how'd 'that' go?
It's done.
Alrighty. Good work, nice.
Once the reward hits, I'll wire it over to 'that account.' Word is the bank there's already watching frequent transfers.
Can we still use personal expenses for the ruse?
What do you think?
Yeah, fair...
We don't have much time left.
But we don't need to think about it just yet.
First, let's go celebrate!

eps.name: Let Us Ring a Ding Ding

lines.x: A breakout from the underground arena, two stages advanced in the preliminaries... a challenger with outstanding skill, Infected knight, 'Wild Mane' Iwona Krukowska...
'Wild Mane?'
What is it, Sona?
...however, caused an improper altercation with other knights in the rest quarters, and was violent in the confrontation...
...may face a 'suspension'...
Here it goes again. Another report on the dark side of Infected knights.
'Improper altercation.' Hah, do you have to imagine? It can't have been anything but losers hooting at the winner.
When it's Infected knights competing, they'll say shit as bad they want to say.
You saw that clip of the match just now, Ashley. This Kuranta's... got some skills.
Her style's pretty distinct from those flower-show nobles, yeah, but she's still a ways off from the top tiers.
Mmm...
You wanna recruit her?
I've heard about her before... some of the knightclub knows her.
What are we still looking for? 'The team leader needs real skill and overpowering force.' Your words, Ashley.
I wanna test our luck. Every independent knight is getting scouted by knightclub agents. If we invite her as an Infected knightclub, that oughta catch her interest.
I've got a feeling she's the one we're looking for.
Are you interested in her skill, or do you think she shares our agenda?
That's what'cha find out in your a little chat.
Firstly, you need a chance to meet and talk at all. You heard it on the news. The K.G.C.C. is considering handing her a 'suspension'. That's not something you settle with a flick of the wrist.
The prelims just started, she's an Infected, and she's green for an independent knight. No one's going to be able to help her.
And what if we actually could, this time?
You need to think this through properly, Sona. It's a risky gamble. If she's already firm on riding this down to the end, we're going to be rivals.
But... if you need to talk to her, it's going to be worth all our strength trying. Where Pinus Sylvestris is right now, every new ally is crucial.
We oughta try and see. If we can get our point across... then things being what they are, I'm pretty sure she'll want to do a little something for the Infected.
Some days later...
Tch. Nobody in the K.G.C.C. wants to hear the facts. If you don't want a fair ruling in the first place, why get all sanctimonious blabbing at me?

Hff... damn, it hurts. Police batons got way more oomph than all that third-rate noble crap. Way I see it, they might as well put those guards up to fight a round or two. Lords in the audiences would love to see that...
She's out, she's out!
Huh?
Wild Mane Knight, does leaving the General Chamber of Commerce mean the government's already made their ruling?
What are your thoughts on the dispute you had with the knights in the lounge? Was it an act of provocation?
Is it fair to the other knights to have you, an Infected knight, using the same facilities?
Get out of my face! All of you!
You need to answer our questions, Wild Mane Knight! Also, just what pretext did you use for the General Chamber of Commerce to let you slip out...
Aaarrrrghhh, who the hell d'you think you are?!
Oh, here she is.
Hah, hah... huh?
Were you... waiting for me?
I saw that scene at the entrance coming a mile away. You only had a few choices, and one of them was here. Unless... you wanted to bust your way out.
Looks like you've got it now though. The further you are from the camera, the better. Let me introduce myself...
No need, I know you. Big cannon-toter, and there's the other one with the sword. You two Zalaks are partners, and you're good enough in a fight.
I'll take that as a compliment.
Ashlock, right? Are you here to pick a fight? Or just make fun of me?
We knew you were walking into a mess. So we came to offer help, in case you need.
Ohh. Such as?
Take those reporters, not to mention some of the agents around with ulterior motives. We can help you turn them all down.
Who are you guys?
I'll take that one.
If you already know us and what we do, then this'll go a lot easier.
We keep a keen eye on the Infected knights. So when we saw on TV the kind of trouble you were getting into, we came up with a little plan...
Could you just come out and say it already? Complicated talk is just gonna hurt my head. What do you want out of me?
To put it straight, we're in charge of the Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub. If you believe in the same things we do, then I want you to join us.
Pinus Sylvestris? Never heard of it.
We're a new knightclub, mostly Infected knights...
...Infected?
Yep.
You think you'll make more money with a club full of Infected knights barely clinging to life? Ha, must be paradise and a half in your head.
No! We're not like the clubs who use Infected as a moneymaking sideshow. We're all about mutual aid between Infected.
And how's that gonna work?
The first part... we'll improve the treatment of Infected knights in the Major. That's the idea.
We all know how the Infected in the other clubs end up. All the money they promise is a scam, and their minimum bet is your life.
Ha. I bet all the Infected who fought their way through the arena want to ask: when's a single day go by without betting your life?
When those noble knight families lose, at least they've still got a home to trot back to with their tails between their legs...
Infected? If you want status, you head into that nightmare of an underground arena with everyone else and lose your mind scrambling for a knight title.
And just suppose you live through it. How long's your career, how far do you go? That's all for the moneybags to decide. They just wanna see you bleed.
Beat you half to death? Not enough for 'em. What those lunatic scumbags want to see in their heart of hearts is—
Wild Mane, let's not.
Ugh. I know. Just venting.
No point talking about all this, anyway. What I need is someplace I can fix my weapon. I can do better than this beat-up hunk of junk shelled lance.
Uh, we may not have a real certified weapon specialist yet, but we could always hire a professional...
In other words, if I join, I get some basic benefits?
Right, salary... we can just about pay you like another knightclub. But, you know, our aim's always been...
Then I'm joining.
Oh?
I said, I'm joining. I don't know what your Infected knightclub's gonna be like, but it's gotta beat any other choice.
I've seen the eyes of the uninfected knights staring at us and I'm through with it. I'm not associating with any of those chuckleheads.
Before I became a knight, survival was top priority, and everything was for getting fed. Ever since becoming a knight... that hasn't changed much.
I don't wanna put up with all that just to eat. If your thing can keep my stomach from running empty, and there's no old noble knightheads to make me throw up, then I'm with you for now.
In that case, you want to get paid how much...?
Uhh...
(Holds out three fingers)
Three?

Money for three days' food, and the funds to fix my weapon. I'm taking that match the day after tomorrow, and when I get that prize money, I'll pay you back, and then I'm officially splitting the rest with you.
The K.G.C.C. wanted bail before they'd let me out. Their stupid fine was so precise, it was my bank balance straight up—
If anyone could get the number, it'd be them.
So, right now, I'm penniless, and I'm starving. No objections there?
None at all. I can give you an advance here, and when you're done with that pressing stuff, come see us at the knightclub.
Now we're talking. Tell me the address. I'm gonna need your base tomorrow so I can practice how I'm gonna bust clean through the armor of that jackass who had words for me—
Alright, I'll spar with you.
Deal, see you there. If you renege on me, I'm coming to find you and you'd better believe I'll settle the score!
What's up, Ashley? You've been eyeing me this whole time.
You seriously... just gave her cash, had her join, and let her go? My guess is she doesn't even get why the K.G.C.C. let her walk out of that building in the first place.
Haha, fixing your weapon's gotta be number one when you're a battle maniac! She's been slammed up for days, and there's no way she's talking serious business first thing.
And besides, we've got no time to explain things to her right now, we just came by with a few words. We've got other stuff going on.
So you're saying you don't think there's a way to wrap this up quick, and you'll be in a better place to chat her up tomorrow?
All we can do is stay on guard. It depends on if they leave the neighborhood Infected alone tonight...
Wishful thinking.
I know. That's exactly why this is something we have to do right now. I'll sort out the stuff with Wild Mane later, trust me.
Fine, whatever. I mean, your instinct wasn't wrong. Wild Mane sure is interesting.
Oh? Where's this coming from?
The way she sees things... is kind of like how I used to. She might be more blunt... but she's got none of that excess baggage.
Pffhaha. That's just what I need, you two finding common ground.
The next day...
An abandoned office building entrance
This is the place, right? This dump... is this seriously a knightclub?
Whatever, just in case. I might as well go in and ask...
Hey, are the Flametail or Ashlock Knights in here—
Looking for the leader?
Yeah, I'm here to check in.
I'm surprised someone actually came knocking... Unfortunately, they just left on business... Why are you carrying luggage?
I'm surprised myself. What's got this place so strapped for cash? You don't even have a sofa?
Award money's ample for knights, true... but Sona and her crew use most of it to settle Infected.
Oh, I see. Thing is, I just got kicked out by my landlord, so I've only got my knightclub for shelter.
Put your luggage down for now, and we can work out what to do once the leader's back.
Speaking of, are they this busy all the time? Where do they drill?
There's a lot of work-related communication that needs handling.
Audits against Knightclubs for Infected knights are getting stricter. The Flametail and Ashlock Knights are constantly talking with the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi and the Grand Knight Territory.
*yawn* That's bureaucrats for you, all image. I thought at least there'd be proper eats for my initiation ceremony. Ashlock sure seemed like she could handle her drink.
Speaking of Ashlock, where's she put her cannon? I've been wanting to check it out. With a 'baaang' like that, and that truckload of destructive power...
Er, her weapon's... probably at home.
You're duping me.
Huh? Nuh-uh...
I can tell. You're hiding something from me.
What, scared to tell me where Flametail and Ashlock are right now? They're up to something outside of the Major for sure. Sensed as much during our conversation last night.
The leader did warn me someone might be coming, but the rest isn't... for me to tell you.
If she doesn't want you telling me, why wouldn't they wait until I came before they left?
They...
Something's definitely pressing on 'em, and that's what's keeping 'em away from here. It can't be anything good.
Ha, I was right!
I knew it. It couldn't have been that simple when they came looking for me. You're up to some risky business, aren't you?
If Flametail and Ashlock made the right call... then they—no, we—want you to lend us your power.
Oh, and there's the other thing... they put up some extra money to rescue you.
...Can you repeat that?
They wouldn't have wanted you to know. Not every member agrees with how our leader's going about things. She saw the news report about you, and decided to try getting you out.
Since the deal even got made, there must have been conditions attached...
Stop. I don't wanna owe 'em favors. If that's really how it's going down, I'm mad as hell, because I've got some responsibility for this too.
Fess up. Where'd they go?
They went to a neighborhood with an Infected settlement, to scatter them before they're taken away.
Wait, Wild Mane!
What now?
Flametail and Ashlock, they're both exceptionally kind, honest knights. I'm certain they want to recruit you because they know you're like us...
They're not just Infected knights, they're people who want to change the lot of the Infected in Kazimierz.
And what does that mean?
When you... see their efforts, you'll understand.
Over there—over there—
Don't worry, just get behind me...
Come out, Infected! You can't run!
*whimper*... I'm sorry... I... I'm not bad... don't take me...
Calm down, I'll keep them away. Are you all hidden, kid?
Yeah... Miss, I—I'm scared... where's... mom and dad...
I'll protect you, just like your mom and dad did. Trust me, okay? Once I call for you to come out, we'll find somewhere new to live, together.
Miss... I want to go home...
Keep quiet a sec.
Mmm...
Right then.
Phew. C'mere—
Yo.
Huh?! You—
...Wild Mane?
Found you this fast and all I had was a wisp of a clue, and I got here quicker than that gray Zalak did. No slouch, am I?
You really did track us down.
You're protecting this kid? He's Infected?
Yep.
What, you're gonna hide a kid here? That's a whole five hunters. If just one of 'em succeeds, then he's lost before you even try to get him back.
We... we're too short-handed right now. This is the most we can do.
Mm-hm. So, this is what you're really up to?
Yeah. That's about the size of it.
You're not just in for Infected knights. You guys are helping out more Infected besides, aren't you?
That's the deal.
Ha... and that's why you only recruit Infected knights. Because only the Infected would sympathize with Infected.
Everyone keeps the same secret, has the same wish, even though it seems... totally head-on with those competition knights, yeah?
Yeah. Wild Mane, I don't have time to give you the whole story right now, so if you want out...
Why? I think this is majorly cool.
Oh?
Yeah, no time. But before anything else, Flametail, I want you to seriously, truly invite me one more time.
You mean... you've decided to do this with us?
Ha. What, am I not just like you?
Alright... I get it.
Wild Mane, are you willing to become a member of the Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub, to aid and be aided as an Infected knight, and to give your all, as we all do, for the sake of the Infected?
Wild Mane? Sorry, I messed that up, didn't I. I never feel like that speech quite works for me...
Call me Iwona.
I'm the Wild Mane Knight, Iwona Krukowska.
Ha. Alright, you're calling me Sona then.
Sure, Sona. I, Iwona, as a new inductee to the Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub, will serve as a knight to seize glory, and to achieve our ideals.
And my ideals—Sona, if Pinus Sylvestris can change the lot of the Infected, and protect the people... then together with you, I'm going for broke.
Going for broke... yeah, that's exactly the right choice of words here.
But it's not time to stake our lives just yet. We can't leave this kid alone. You're the leader, and it'll be too risky if anyone sees you in an alley doing this stuff.
Just get the kid out of here for now, and I'll handle things here.
Watch close, see what I'm really capable of—wait, no, you oughta go first. Watch another time, then!
Get out of here! By the time you've got the kid dropped off safe, I'll probably be on my way back to find you.
Got it. Don't make this a scene though. Settle it slick, and we'll be waiting for you at the real Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub camp!
No sweat.
Phew... kept'cha waiting, huh, Ashley...
Good going, Sona. All done?
For the time being. Say, where's Iwona... uh, Wild Mane?
Seems she went off reminiscing with Jamie...
Here you were, I got back forever ago. Wanted to ask Jamie for a drink, but there's no good booze anywhere around here.
How's this, then. You get the prize money, and I'll treat you to a round.
Ha, done.
I'll join too then.
Nah.
'Scuse me? Why won'tcha let me in on this?! What, you guys already have stuff to talk behind my back?
Do you even have time? There's a mountain of paperwork from the Knights Association...
Agh... don't remind me.
We'll just be belly-aching the whole time, and you hate that stuff. Besides, you're not that good with liquor, and you don't like staying at bars all night.
Ugh... no lie. If that's what you're planning, you're your own.
Right, Iwona, if you're not busy, there's a few things I need to tell you about, regarding a few projects Pinus Sylvestris has going...
Those, yeah? Greynuty just told me about 'em.
Rescuing Infected, and Infected knights, and using prize money to help more Infected get legal identities...
Huh, you're all wised up already... so, what'cha think?
All the prize money I earn can go to you guys. I was training to become a campaign knight. Never thought about doing this to earn big bucks. If it wasn't for... ugh.
Whatever. But in terms of the, uh, more detailed plans, I didn't actually get any of it.
Greynuty said, what Pinus Sylvestris is gonna do for the Infected, how we should do it... you do all that planning.
So I get it now. You can think this stuff through and put it into practice. That's mad mighty.
Mmm—it's not all that mighty, heh...
I've been thinking about doing something for the Infected forever... just like the Blood Knight! He does some good, fine stuff.
The Blood Knight reformed the Major's regulations, but us... Pinus Sylvestris looks to bring together a big group, form an even greater bloc, and do even more for the Infected.
Then I'm definitely part of it.
So it's settled, I'll follow you all the way. I believe you're what's right, and whatever bigger challenges come, I'll be there to help you deal with 'em.
But, if you can help me sort out a few problems of my own first, I'll worship you all the more.
Heheh. If it'll win more trust, of course I'm happy to do it. What'cha need helping with?
Uh... what's this?
My pet.
Peeehhh... pet?
A pet, huh... that's... it's pretty cute! Iwona, how'd you come across it?
Ha, just saw it in a dump by the roadside, felt like I could get some use. They say you know a reliable engineer or some sort?
Engineer? I don't know what kind you're talking about...
Oh, I think there are a few in the neighborhood who know their machine stuff.
Just a bit ago you were having hell finding a weapons repair guy, but now you've got people who understand these machines? What's the deal?
Haha... well, he might take an interest in your weapon too. Should I take you?
Whatever, I'll take it over myself. You and Greynuty have other stuff to talk about, so go get chattering. Where's that engineer live?
...I see now. This fine outer hull and internal design...
How is it? Good stuff, right? This weapon's been with me for years now.
But something I don't get... if you really are that strong, why was your major lance's hull so broken when you were coming out of the K.G.C.C.?
You wanna know?
So long as you don't blow up the story too big, I'll hear you out. I wanna know how you use it, principally.
Ha. Where should I start, then—
Back when I was still readying to be a campaign knight, this lance was drawn up for the real battlefield, designed to open the way for the forces behind.
My major lance takes a tremendous amount of force and impact, multiple times over.
After the way's opened up, I'd switch over to a minor lance.
But, ever since I started in the arena, I've never let this big thing break.
In other words... you haven't used your full strength, because you don't actually want to take anyone's life?
In those grimy underground arenas...

Whether it's orders blaring at top volume or captions on the screen, it's all pushing me to do vicious work on someone, and they don't call it a win until they see every other Infected on the ground...
I never once seriously injured 'em before.
But when I finally clawed my way to being a competition knight, I heard what those old nobleheads were saying about me in the lounge...
Well, I hadn't treaded the ground in a long, long time. Took out my minor lance, aimed it right at the armor over those acid-spitters' hearts—
You understand? I was really, actually hoping to run them straight through.
Cheers to that. Would've been feel-good!
Feel-good nothing. Just give my pet a look already!
Oh, right...
Examination's done. This is an old Raythean model... and it shouldn't be too complicated to reboot it.
But it's got fairly limited capability. It probably won't understand your commands too well.
I'm good as long as it knows its own name.
Name? What will you name it?
I decided a long time ago—I'm calling it Justice Knight. Here's hoping it's a little doer of a pet.
Its original function was cover fire, it seems.
Well, isn't that peachy? It'll clean up nobodies for me. More boring than I'd like for a function, though. Can you make it more useful?
Depends whether there's any other compatible modules it can make use of...
You need to find other parts to fit? Leave it for later, then. Let's see if we can get to light up first?
Taking a look now...
(Sound of machine activating)
Ooh, there it goes.

lines.st: Climbing the haystack again, Justyna?
You're looking that way again? At the Grand Knight Territory?
I know the Grand Knight Territory. That's where the powerful knight lords compete against each other, right?
You never stop talking about it, of course I know!
Hey, Justyna, you're really planning to go there?

I know you've been practicing whenever you don't have farm work. Everybody in the village knows you want to be a knight. You can fight off the bandits who come looking for trouble, they're no match for you.
But the Grand Knight Territory is pretty far... If you go, will you ever come back?
You will? Really? After you become a knight?
It's a promise then. We'll wait for you to come home, showered in glory, and then we'll spread the word that our town is the home of the Justyna, the knight!

lines.x: No footprints, no tracks at all...
They didn't go this way.
The hospital team lost the target, they're bound to pass through here if they're trying to escape.
Stay alert. I want regular reports.
Roger.
Hm?
Guh... agh...
Go.
They're going to know something's wrong. Come with me while there's still time.
I don't take orders from Infected.
Though I admit you have some skill, able to handle those lowlifes from that distance.
But don't think that means I'm here to listen to you.
The Armorless have this whole place surrounded. You're not getting out of here alone.
They're not going to give up the chase.
So what?
I'll make them pay for it.
That's not going to be easy.
Your wounds haven't healed, how can you hide from them like this?
Ugh... damned Armorless.
I'll go along with you for now, but only because we share a common enemy. I'll make those murderers pay for what they did!
We'll talk about that later, we're still in danger here.
Don't think I have anything else to say to you later.
Listen girly, you've got a certain champion to thank for the fact that you're standing here, speaking to me in that armor.
You're not wrong.
There was a time when I had bottomless gratitude for the Blood Knight.
Hm?
Why so quiet?
Competition.
I watched you go up against Maria Nearl—'Plastic' Szewczyk.
I don't like you.
You're slightly less obnoxious out on the field.
Your feelings don't matter, but watch the attitude. You should never have taken me out of the hospital.
It was Sona's idea. And anyway—
And anyway nothing. Sona is your leader's name? Hmph, sounds like another little girl.
She's a friend.
I'll take you back to the base. Sona and my other friends are all waiting for us.
Make no mistake, I may have agreed to work with you for now, but I'm not part of your group, infected knight.
No, I'm very clear, you're not at all like us.
But, 'Plastic' Szewczyk, you must know, you have no hope of resisting the Armorless Union alone.
Heh... Sure, you're right about that.
It's funny, Kazimierz as it is today. An Infected can be a knight, and a gang of thugs like the Armorless can roam about freely.
Funny...
Let's meet up with the others first.
We won't get anything done by ourselves.
Ten days earlier...
Just who the hell are you people?!
There's no end to them...
Hiding in the crowd, are you?!
Don't think I won't—
Wait! Don't do it!
Ah... looks like I'm a little late? How did this turn into such a warzone?
Not good. Not good at all.
Anyway, let's pick up the pace. Start evacuating people first.
That's all we can do.
It's you...
Hey there, 'Fartooth' Knight, I still haven't had a chance to congratulate you on your title. Nice job there.
I saw a piece on you. Popularity's through the roof, huh?
Announcing a new knightclub, getting real controversial, pushing that popularity up, hm? Hmph, looks like you've kicked a hornet's nest to me.
Infected knights...
That's enough of that.
Ugh...
Alright, anyway, the fight's fought, what's done is done. *Sigh*.
Flametail, and Ashlock... What are you doing here?
Do you know who these people are?
Like the back of my hand. But this isn't a good place for a chat... these guys are looking to make a scene, and the media will be here before long. That wouldn't end well for you.
And anyway, you're probably hurt, right?
Just come with us, all nice and quiet-like, and we'll explain nice and slow what's going on. What do you say?
Do you have to say it like you're kidnapping her?
Huh? Did I?
A little.
I'll go. I trust you.
Sorry, what?
Just take me along, wherever you're going. I trust you.
I feel like...
We're the same. I can trust you.
Where are we...?
Oh! That was fast. Your wounds all better?
Yes.
Are all these people here Infected?
Sure are. Everyone who lives here is. Most got unlucky, got sick in an accident or something, lost their jobs and homes, ended up with nowhere to go.
Granted, the room and board here is a little rough, but... it's better than nothing, right?
Sona, Iwona's in trouble, I'll go see what I can do.
The Armorless Union is probably still active. I'll tell the others to be careful too.
Alright. Don't worry, I'm on it.
Right. You've got it.
She...
Hm? Oh, you mean Ashley? She's a good girl. The way she talks is just a little too straight up sometimes.
She hasn't said a word to me since we first met.
Ahaha! Ashley's a little shy with strangers too.
*Sigh*. Well, alright...
Ashley just thinks you're too different from us, like you've walked a different path.
I mean, you're a star of the Major, with a shiny new knight title, so hot right now!
No matter which way you look at it, you've got a bright future!
No... I don't.
An Infected... an Infected knight... has no future.
That doesn't sound like something a big competition winner would say.
I used to think that as long as I could be a knight, there was still hope. If I could win in the arena, maybe...
Maybe you could even earn some respect, be seen as a true knight, and win back the life you used to have?
Yes... That's what I thought.
But that's not how it works. An Infected knight is still Infected. And that's all that matters.
Yeah. That's pretty much what I've said to Ashley.
And besides, so what if you're a knight? Just look what happened to you back there in that crowd. Those folks who jumped you didn't care if you're Infected or noble or whatever.
You were saying they're with the Armorless Union?
Knight-Assassins... the words feel gross in my mouth.
Yeah, that's them.
They sell themselves to the General Chamber of Commerce, bumping off knights who don't play ball... They call themselves experts in handling knights, and wear that with pride.
And add on the rewards, in cheers and treasure, that the audience heaps upon the knights, proper or not, how much dignity is left in the title? That's before you even talk about us.
So me getting attacked this time, that was the K.G.C.C.'s orders?
Probably. You've got the spotlight on you, and you're turning down all the knightclub invitations, and talking like you do out there... sooner or later, you get a knock like that at your door.
And if you won't be their goldmine, then they find you a 'dishonorable' exit, so you won't get in the way of their business.
So you know, not that crazy you'd get attacked.
Two three-week periods.
Huh?
On my way to the Grand Knight Territory, I got infected.
And since I was Infected, they wouldn't let me register as a knight. So I wandered around for three weeks with nowhere to stay.
Hmm...
It was during the third week that the Blood Knight won the Major.
Then, three weeks later, they opened the registration window for Infected knights.
I didn't have any money left on me, so I went to the black market ring, working up enough money to pay the registration fees.
That... couldn't have been easy.
Underground knight sports for infected knights is pretty much the same as the black market.
From the underground competitions to the Major, I've seen too many ridiculous things to be surprised anymore.
Haha! Fair enough. There's definitely stuff going on in the underground fights that tops whatever the Armorless Union can come up with.
We've all been through stuff like that, more or less, and that's how we end up here.
There sure are a lot of Infected here.
Right... how can I put it? You can make a lot in the knight competitions, at least enough to buy a place for these people who have nowhere else to go.
So that means the money's still worth something, right?
Right.
Heh. Nice.
You're easier to get along with than we might've thought. When I first bumped into you, I was worried you were all icy cold.
Oh? You got all quiet?
I don't know...
I just... I just don't know... I guess I don't know what to say at times like this.
Ugh.
Alright... I think I get it now. You and Ashley are both shy with strangers.
And this habit of yours where you say half a thing up front, I think I'm getting used to it. Not that there's anything wrong with it, it's just tricky to follow sometimes.
Either way, thanks for helping me.
Ah, it was nothing. Don't sweat it.
So anyway, where are you gonna go? If you pop right back out, they're gonna go after you again.
That scuffle back there is gonna get reported soon. What do you think we'll see on the news?
No idea. Whatever they want.
Honestly... I don't know where I'd go. I haven't had anywhere to go for a long time.
Hmm...
Well, you may think I'm just throwing it out there to hold it over you, but this is an honest offer, out of the kindness of my heart.
There's me, and Ashley, and Iwona, you've seen her, with the big loud mouth?
Yes, I know her.
The truth is... we had the same idea as you, in your little statement about setting up an independent knightclub.
I came up with the name already. Pinus Sylvestris. The Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub, a club of our very own.
And since you've got nowhere to go, Justyna, wouldn't you consider maybe joining us?
You're... inviting me?
Are you sure?
Sure as sure can be. Can I spell it out any clearer for you?
Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub...
Alright. I'm in.
—Us... together.

lines.st: Justyna, are you going now?
Play your flute again, won't you? Whatever you want. Everyone loves hearing you play.
Beautiful.
We won't get to hear that again for a long time. Everyone's gonna miss you.
Aren't you worried about going so far all alone? We know you get lonely, you're just too shy to admit it. Always sitting quietly off to the side.
You think the wind might carry your voice back home?
You're going to be a knight. We all believe you're going to be an amazing one, like that Radiant Knight you're so fond of.
Good luck, Justyna.
Don't be a stranger now.

lines.x: Now that we're here, we're safe. Someone will meet us out front.
How are your wounds?
Fine. Just a scratch, barely worth mentioning. But you Infected knights, you base yourselves here in the city? Not out in a village somewhere?
No one would allow the Infected to live within the city limits. Just what are you up to?
Nothing.
When you've got money, you can do anything.
Hah... money. True enough. So long as you have money, you can do anything here.
But if word gets out, you'll be arrested.
Anyone eager to open their mouth gets a word with my arrows first.
I won't allow anyone to harm the Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub.
Then lead the way. I can't stand the thought of the Armorless Union scum on the loose out there. I'm working against the General Chamber of Commerce now, so I need to move fast.
Show me to your Infected knight leader, who forced one of your strength to drag me out of the hospital against your will.
Not against my will.
What?
It wasn't Sona who sent me to rescue you. It was my own choice. Not at all against my will.
But how? I understand we have a mutual distaste for one another.
Or is this some kind of big picture play?
No.
It's just... you have a family.
What are you talking about?!
Those insects! They dare move against my boy?!
Don't worry, Sona has eyes on him. The Armorless backed off. They didn't touch your son.
Then I must offer my thanks.
You owe them to Sona. It was all her idea.
As for me, my personal feelings towards you have nothing to do with whether or not I'll let you get hurt.
I just... your son...
Without you, he'd have nowhere to go.
Justyna the Knight has a home, with family and friends waiting for her.
But Justyna the Infected doesn't.
An Infected has nowhere to go.
No, it's not just the Infected. Here, in this place, there are a lot more people with nowhere to go.
It's too late for me. I can't go back.
If the wind really can carry my voice home...
Then I'll have to ask it not to. Don't carry anything but the sound of my flute.
Let Justyna stay a simple, ordinary girl... dreaming of knighthood.

lines.st: On what will you shine your light? On what can you shine your light?
To do your utmost. That is the glory that all knights should seek.
Glory. Glory did not betray Kazimierz. It is the people that abandoned glory.
That does not mean glory is meaningless.
The word glory itself is far too intangible, Margaret. Wearing it on one's sleeve is foolish.
That is why knights must prove themselves by their deeds alone.
Suppose the cause to which you've dedicated your life turns to sneer at all that you are... Do your deeds still hold meaning?

lines.x: Ngh...!
Uncle!
How many times I have told you? Never speak to your opponent in combat.
Then why won't you use your Arts?
I should ask you myself. Your Arts have improved. You must have realized the truth, then?
About that... yes.
However...! Uncle Młynar, right now, I would prefer it if you—
Say no more.
You sent my sword flying. That means you've won, Margaret.
Even though... you never used your Arts at all?
It was a match, nothing more, nothing less. All other factors are mere excuses. When have I ever taught you to be so indecisive?
If you've come to understand the price your grandfather paid for your naivety, then you must understand the meaning of your return.
Margaret, you are trampling my father's will.
For us to become cowards?
'Fear neither hardship nor darkness.'
Right. Fear neither hardship nor darkness... You won the battle. You won the chance to continue your mistakes.
Do remember to say your prayers, and pray that your stubbornness won't drag too many souls into its wake.
Uncle...
Do you still believe those times are forever behind us?
Margaret, how dare you ask me this question?
I have a telephone meeting later. Everyone is busy with the Major. As for your little charade... Try not to do anything to distract me from my responsibilities.
Face reality. See how others must live. See their needs. And stop imposing your fantasies on them for your own glory.
You find yourself disappointed, sooner or later.
But what if I don't end up like you?
Like me...? You?
Looks like those Sarkaz friends of yours took excellent care of you during your exile.
Margaret!
Margaret, are you okay...?
Yes... I'm fine.
Okay, Maria. Stop squeezing her like that. Her arms must be going numb.
Oh! S-Sorry... Does it hurt?
*Sigh*. I never imagined I would find you two dueling today—
—Młynar hits hard, doesn't he?
How has he been the past few years?

Normal. He's been living a pretty normal, routine life. He heads out early for work and comes home late, always busy with meetings or dealing with difficult clients, and, once in a while, he gets into debates on account of the 'Nearl' name.
But I'm sure this 'normal' feels enough like a punishment for him.
I mean, he used to be so... Uh, never mind! Let's talk about something else!
Look at how you frown. You won, but it doesn't feel like it, does it? Seems that stubbornness runs in the family.
At least Uncle Młynar's agreed to let Margaret compete in the Major, right?
If she takes the crown again, I'm sure that'll be the end of his complaints.
After, it's all for—

lines.st: For what?

lines.x: For family. For myself. For... a knight's glory.
To tell the truth, I know the bounty of the land of the knights today is a far cry from the glory of the past.
Maria? Why so quiet all of a sudden?
Ah...
Are you hungry?
A-Auntie!
Let's give Margaret some time to rest.
...Hello?
My apologies. I just got home, and I had some family matters to deal with... Not a problem. The documents have been annotated.
Sponsoring the Major...? Me? No, please, stop pulling my leg. You know I'm not suited to something like that...
Right... Of course. It's work. I shouldn't be trying to bargain. My apologies.
Yes, of course. I will be there right away.
The window's open, Toland.
You're getting rusty. I could sense your presence.
But, please, come in through the front door next time.
Okay... Doesn't look like you're hurt.
He held back.
Yeah... He didn't use his Arts after all.
And actually, it's been a long time since he had that look on his face...
—Alright! Get up.
So what's your plan now? You need to report to the Knights Association, don't you?
There is a complicated process, but it's not time yet.
Margaret... We barged into that unfinished match... Is it really going to be alright?
Alright? You're looking at an exiled Infected, Maria. She smashed through the arena walls and waltzed onto the field, remember?
With all that she has done, what's interrupting a match or two?
R-Right... You have a point...
Trouble is an old friend. Be it then or now, so long as Kazimierz remains as it is, we will face all manner of obstacle in our path.
So are you going to compete in the Major, Margaret?
I am.
Is that... something that you absolutely have to do?
...Maria.
It's just... you finally got to come home... I don't think the Association would make things difficult for Uncle and the family...
But if the Chamber of Commerce have their eyes on you again like they did a few years ago, then we—
Maria has a point, Margaret.
It doesn't matter how the Knights Association deals with this, or what the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi's decision is. To the masses and the knights, you are still the Radiant Knight.
I have to admit... I don't get it either. I don't understand why you insist on competing again... I don't understand what you're thinking.
But I'm sure you have it figured out. Stubbornness may run in the family, but that's not the same thing as recklessness.
This is just the beginning...
Whether I take the crown or not... it's just a beginning.
Maria, Auntie Zofia. If all we do is cower on the sidelines and look on at society with indifference, if we only comfort ourselves, insisting that nothing can be done—
—then when will we truly regain our glory?
Are the knights of Kazimierz destined to wallow forever in times long past?
So you want to...
Shining light on the tragedies of the world is not a permanent commitment to dispelling the darkness.
Sometimes... Sometimes, it's just to illuminate the path forward, to help those who are lost see things from a new perspective.
Even in today's Kazimierz, there is still meaning to keeping our faith.
Margaret...
Alright... Sounds like you've made up your mind.
But you put on such a show as soon as you got back to Kazimierz. If there's a chance... Margaret.
Don't forget you're home. You have family and memories here. You have Maria... and me.
At the very least, give Maria some time to fawn on you.
A-Auntie! What are you saying?!
She's been working really hard lately, you know?
I-I haven't...
Yes, I know. I'm proud of you, Maria. You've really... really grown up.
Maria Nearl, you will be an excellent knight.
Oh... right.
About that... Margaret. If you're thinking of joining the Major, you probably won't have time to rack up the points you need, right?
It will be difficult, but not impossible.
Right... Just like the Candle Knight who's been popular the past few years. I understand it didn't even take her a month to earn enough points.
That may be the case, but the Candle Knight racked up her points early in the preliminaries. Even if Margaret gains the Knights Association's approval right away, we'd still be cutting it close.
What's your plan?
The competition's rules have changed, but the basics are still the same. So long as I scrape together a win in the melees, I should still stand a chance.
But... that's too risky! I drew a lot of attention when I stepped into the ring. If you take to the arena yourself, you're bound to attract even greater opponents...!
Right, and we can't say for sure that the corporations won't pull their dirty tricks on you.
Anyway, we need to come up with a plan as soon...
I... I have an idea.
Maria?
Independent knights... like me and Margaret... aren't part of a knightclub, so we compete as part of a 'knight family,' right?
That's true... You used the Nearl family coat of arms during your matches.
And, if I remember correctly, it's not just knights of the same knightclub who are allowed to transfer their points, right? Those from the same knight family are allowed to do the same?
—Maria?!
No, I can't accept that.
Maria, those points are proof of your gallantry as a knight. I can't—
—I don't care!
I... I chose to become a knight because I wanted our home to be ours.
The Nearl family's ancestors... Grandfather... Father... and you. I didn't want to lose all that. I wanted to protect the Nearls' glory.
But... But then I realized... I was too naive about all this.
If we followed them into the Major, if we became their knights just to uphold our knightly name... then... we wouldn't have changed anything.
Margaret...
...I don't actually want to become a knight.
Knights can't change the knighthood, Margaret.
Maria...
If there is something only you can do, I beg you to do it.
I... I just want this home to still be our home...
I understand, but I still need some time to think.
Alright now... Don't be so gloomy. We've got lots of ways to go about this.
Why don't we go to Old Marcin's bar for a drink later? It's been such a long time. I'm sure he wants to talk to you.
What about Uncle...?
You should try not to bother him. I'll check up on him.
There's one thing, Margaret.
There are a lot more Infected in the city than before, and the Armorless Union is also much more active.
Be careful.
Report.
What's the situation?
The Radiant Knight and Młynar Nearl just fought each other in a duel.
I read their lips. Neither looked very happy.
*Sigh*... I seriously hope she'll just stay home and spend some time with her family. They haven't seen each other in so long.
Please don't get yourself mixed up in anything you shouldn't, Radiant Knight. I don't want any more trouble to deal with.
Uh, Platinum, so what should we do...?
Keep an eye on her.
If she tries anything, let me know right away.
Of course.
What are the others up to? Mission-wise?
Uh... Per the rules...
I'm not allowed to know.
Whatever. It's either something about the Infected or the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi.
As long as we don't have to lift a finger, it's all fine by me.
Młynar...
Młynar!
...Do you need something?
Are you... okay?
Margaret might've stopped herself in that last duel, but you weren't wearing your helmet...
You needn't worry about me.
She caught you by surprise... didn't she?
She caught all of us by surprise. After all... she's become far stronger than any of us could've imagined.
Is that all you have to say...?
I'm worried about you, Młynar.
I've fallen so far that it falls on you to worry about me.
Right. I'm sure even you have realized.
It's just a sprain, nothing more... Her swordsmanship has come a long way, but she's not yet at the point where I can't handle her.
But... hmm.
You're wondering what made Margaret like this?
Of course, there are her Sarkaz friends. They are all... noble souls. Shockingly noble.
It might just be that she's gone through more over these past few years than either of us could imagine.
Maybe... you could swallow your pride just a little and talk to either of them?
Confident enough to order me around now, are you? Did coaching Maria give you such a sense of fulfillment?
Before Margaret became the center of attention, you and your brother were heroes to all of us.
I know how you see the rest of us. Marcin does as well, and so do V and Kowal. Though... at the same time... I am just as disappointed in you.
Let's leave it there. Have fun with work.
Looks like someone used to live here...
Right, there are still traces of their lives... but... are they hiding?
This is... the third settlement we've come across.
In a glittering city, the Infected always run to cold, cramped corners like these.
It's hard to believe... How could Kazimierz be this heartless...?
Are these the Infected Amiya and the kind old knight talked about?
Right... If even the knights knew about this, then things can't be that simple.
Do you still have any strength left, Nightingale? It's getting late. We can head back for some rest, if you need.
No, I can hang on for a little longer.
Okay...
In that case—
Ah—What's the noise?
A wrecking crew... They're tearing down the buildings... and the subterranean structures beneath.
What if there are still Infected hiding below?
Remember everything you see, Liz.
Perhaps Nearl knows something about this.
The Radiant Knight walks down the hallway in silence.
The portraits of past champions line the hallway like fences on the side of the road, filling her entire path with their glory.
Maria...
Hm?
You... Are you... the Radiant Knight?
My apologies. Is this place not open yet?
Oh, uh... No... More accurately, it's not open to the public yet, though I am not sure you count as the public...
Right, sorry about this.
Uh... Forgive me, I haven't yet introduced myself.
Please, call me Malkiewicz. I am the spokes—I am the staff member in charge of maintaining the wall of champions.
Forgive me for asking, Radiant Knight, but what are your plans right now?
Radiant Knight, huh? It wasn't until today that I finally regained that title.
So the Association gave their approval... Congratulations.
Malkiewicz comes back to his senses. The two of them turn their heads forward, to a portrait of the Radiant Knight herself.
Though she stands right next to him—or perhaps precisely because she's right next to him, a strange feeling washes over Malkiewicz.
The stubbornness is finally starting to loosen, and those things that are locked in place will soon be shattered.
You look much more gallant in person.
It's been a few years, after all.
